name: Enablon EHS & Sustainability API
what_it_does: Enablon (Wolters Kluwer) REST API for enterprise environment, health, safety (EHS) and sustainability management platform. Enables AI agents to manage incident reporting and investigation workflow automation for safety management, handle permit-to-work and safety observation data collection from operational sites, access environmental monitoring and compliance tracking for permit and regulatory management, retrieve audit and inspection findings management for corrective action tracking, manage risk assessment and hazard identification data for safety culture programs, handle ESG and sustainability metrics reporting for GHG emissions and energy tracking, access operational risk and process safety management data for industrial operations, retrieve regulatory compliance status and deadline tracking across jurisdictions, manage contractor safety management and prequalification data, and integrate EHS data with SAP, Oracle, and enterprise ERP systems.
use_cases: Processing safety incident reports from EHS automation agents
Tracking environmental permit compliance from regulatory agents
Monitoring audit findings from corrective action management agents
Aggregating ESG metrics from sustainability reporting agents
not_for: Simple HR safety without industrial EHS and process safety management
Financial ESG reporting without operational EHS data collection
Consumer safety without enterprise industrial operations context
best_when: An industrial, manufacturing, or energy company using Enablon wants AI agents to automate incident reporting, permit tracking, audit management, ESG metrics aggregation, and regulatory compliance across operational sites.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: EHS incident data and process safety information are regulated in many jurisdictions — automated incident routing without proper notification chains can violate OSHA and regulatory reporting requirements. Permit-to-work automation without safety review can authorize hazardous work. EHS data contains sensitive worker health information requiring privacy protections.

auth.notes: Enablon uses OAuth 2.0 authentication. Per-tenant credentials. Part of Wolters Kluwer portfolio (acquired 2016). Webhooks for workflow event notifications. REST API with JSON. On-premise and cloud deployment options. SAP integration certified. Strong in oil & gas, chemicals, and manufacturing verticals. Paris, France origin.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; module count, sites, and users determine pricing
pricing.notes: Paris, France. Founded 1999. Acquired by Wolters Kluwer (2016). EHS and sustainability software market. 1,000+ enterprise customers including Shell, Total, and Michelin. 73 countries. Strong in process industries (oil, gas, chemicals). Competes with Sphera and Intelex for industrial EHS market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: Automated incident routing must follow configured notification chains; skipping EHS notification requirements violates OSHA and regulatory mandates
Permit-to-work authorization — never automate permit-to-work issuance without safety officer review; automated PTW bypasses human safety verification for hazardous work
Worker health data privacy — EHS incident data includes medical and health information; ensure HIPAA/GDPR compliant handling before automated processing
On-premise deployment option — some customers run Enablon on-premise; API endpoint and network access differ from cloud deployment
Module licensing — Enablon is modular; API access to incidents, permits, audits, and ESG may require separate module licenses
Regulatory reporting deadlines — environmental permit exceedance and injury reporting have strict deadlines; automated notification failures have regulatory consequences
agent_readiness.security_notes: Industrial EHS and sustainability. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Safety incident, permit, and worker health data.

security_score_components.security_notes: EHS and sustainability. SOC2, GDPR, ISO14001. OAuth2. US/EU. Safety incident and environmental data.
